mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and durable demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS is in the top quartile and FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts coinciding with low unemployment indicate synchronized strength in construction activity and labor markets, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive returns when HOUST exceeds 1.5M and UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product growth reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate profit margins, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal becomes negative when real GDP growth exceeds 4.0% annually.
